events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Scenario: primary/secondary landed reversed on transformers dry-type & pad-mount, rough-complete. This is a defect: reversing primary and secondary terminations can cause overvoltage on secondary side, damage equipment, safety hazard, violates NEC 110.3(B) installation per listing/manufacturer, Art 450 transformer requirements, 250.30 grounding. Decision fail. Risk high or critical? Reversed primary/secondary can energize secondary with primary voltage, potential fire/equipment damage, but not yet energized. Critical maybe because if energized catastrophic. But at rough-complete not yet energized, high. However classification: primary/secondary landed reversed is critical because transformer could be destroyed and cause arc flash/fire upon energization. Need choose. The prompt says if dangerous or non-compliant decision fail. Risk high likely. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete rough-complete maybe 80. Value 0. Sound etc default. Findings: primary and secondary conductors terminated on wrong transformer bushings/terminals. Actions: de-energize (if energized), verify nameplate ratings and terminal designations, remove and re-land conductors per manufacturer, perform continuity/turns ratio/insulation resistance tests, re-inspect. Rationale. References NEC Art 450, 250.30, 110.3(B). Need JSON only. Ensure valid JSON.

  "percent_complete": 80,

  "findings": [
    "Primary and secondary conductors are landed on the wrong transformer terminals/bushings.",
    "Reversed terminations will cause incorrect voltage transformation and can damage connected equipment or the transformer.",
    "Installation does not match the manufacturer’s nameplate, terminal markings, or listing requirements."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Confirm transformer is de-energized and locked out before rework.",
    "Remove and re-land primary and secondary conductors per the manufacturer’s nameplate and terminal designations.",
    "Verify correct phasing, polarity, and grounding connections.",
    "Perform turns-ratio, continuity, and insulation-resistance testing before energization.",
    "Schedule re-inspection and third-party testing as required."
  ],
  "rationale": "Landing the primary and secondary conductors in reverse violates the manufacturer’s installation instructions and NEC transformer and grounding requirements. If energized, the transformer would operate outside its designed configuration, creating a serious fire, equipment-damage, and shock hazard. The installation must be corrected and verified before acceptance.",
  "references": [
    "NEC Art. 450",
    "NEC 250.30",
    "NEC 110.3(B)"
  ]
}
